title: aira capillaris var. tenorei
intro: Aira capillaris var. tenorei, also known as Tenore's Hair-grass or Aira tenorei, is a subspecies of the Aira capillaris plant, a type of grass native to Europe, North Africa, and parts of Asia. Traditionally, Aira capillaris var. tenorei has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which have been reported to help with wound healing, skin issues, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Aira capillaris var. tenorei are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and supporting digestive function. Aira capillaris var. tenorei can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional folk medicine.
